evaluation.manager_summary: Shannon handled a straightforward alcohol treatment inquiry from David but missed multiple fundamentals. She jumped directly into questions without building rapport, collected incomplete insurance information, conducted minimal clinical screening (no withdrawal or severity assessment), made an unverified coverage statement, and closed with vague next steps. The call was transactional and lacked the warmth and thoroughness expected for intake.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent launched directly into intake questions ('How much are you drinking?') within 10 seconds, with no acknowledgment of the caller's situation or emotional state. The tone was transactional throughout, with no use of the caller's name after introduction and no acknowledgment of the difficulty of seeking help.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Start by acknowledging the difficulty of making this call and thanking them for reaching out before moving to intake questions. Use their name and slow down to build trust.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent collected carrier (Summit Select) and member ID but did not obtain the group number when caller said they didn't have it. More importantly, agent did not frame verification as helpful or pivot constructively—just said 'we'll try to look it up' and moved on.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Capture all required fields (carrier, plan type, member ID, group number). If caller doesn't have group number, explain why it's needed and offer to wait while they find it or get it later with clear follow-up.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent asked only the most basic questions (how much, prior treatment) but failed to capture critical severity indicators like last drink, withdrawal symptoms, or frequency beyond 'every night.' No co-occurring mental health screening. The screening was superficial.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Ask about last use, withdrawal symptoms, medical concerns, and whether mental health issues are present. These severity indicators are essential for proper triage and care planning.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent asked about timing ('are you looking to come in soon or still deciding?') which is a basic urgency probe. However, no deeper exploration of urgency signals was conducted—no questions about withdrawal risk, medical safety, or current state despite multi-year daily drinking pattern.
Caller is the patient themselves (David calling for his own treatment).

Caller raised two objections: nervousness about treatment with kids at home, and cost concerns. Agent addressed the kids concern factually but without acknowledging the emotional component. For cost, agent gave unverified reassurance ('Your insurance should cover most of it') without acknowledging the concern first—a compliance and engagement issue.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Acknowledge the concern first ('I understand cost is important'), then explain the verification process. For emotional objections like nervousness, validate the feeling before offering solutions.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent set a vague next step ('someone will call you') with unclear timing ('within the next day or two'). No specific appointment was scheduled, no ownership was assigned (who will call?), and caller had to ask multiple follow-up questions to get basic clarity.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Set a specific callback time window, identify who will call, and explain what that call will accomplish. Confirm the best number to reach them.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent made an unhedged coverage statement ('Your insurance should cover most of it') before verification was complete—a scope overreach that creates expectations without verification. This is a clear compliance violation as it provides coverage assurance an intake coordinator cannot guarantee.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Never make coverage statements before verification. Say 'We'll verify your benefits and let you know exactly what's covered—our benefits team will call you with details.'

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Okay, sure. How much are you drinking?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Every night. Okay. Do you have insurance?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: We can work around schedules. Our outpatient programs allow people to be home in the evenings.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I don't have that with me right now.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: That's okay. We'll try to look it up. Anything else about your situation I should know?

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Yes, Summit Select.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Okay, sure. How much are you drinking?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Got it. Any prior treatment programs?

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I mean, it's been going on for a few years. I tried to cut back on my own but it didn't really work.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Okay. And are you looking to come in soon or still deciding?

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: A lot. Pretty much every night. A six-pack or more.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Within the next day or two.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I think I want to do it but I'm nervous about it. I have kids, so I'm wondering how that works.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: That's good to know. I'm worried about the cost too.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Your insurance should cover most of it. We'll verify.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: We'll have someone call you to set something up.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: When?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Within the next day or two.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Your insurance should cover most of it. We'll verify.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: That's good to know. I'm worried about the cost too.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: That's okay. We'll try to look it up. Anything else about your situation I should know?
